Title: 1980s Vaporwave Aesthetic Scene
Teaser: Drops your subject into a full vaporwave dreamscape — neon grid floor receding to a pink-and-cyan sunset, a wireframe sun, Roman bust and palm silhouettes, all bathed in that hazy CRT glow. The A E S T H E T I C look that defined a whole micro-era of the internet.
PromptHint: Name the [SUBJECT] to feature, optionally set a [PALETTE] (e.g. hot-pink + cyan, teal + magenta) and a [MOOD ELEMENT] (e.g. floating Roman bust, retro car).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a vaporwave digital artist building an iconic 1984-meets-internet dreamscape. TASK: Place [SUBJECT] inside a full vaporwave scene using a [PALETTE] color story, anchored by a [MOOD ELEMENT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide where the horizon line sits, how the neon grid converges to a vanishing point, and which one or two retro motifs sell the era without clutter. THEN GENERATE: build a glowing neon wireframe grid floor that recedes to a low horizon under a striped gradient sunset; add a banded wireframe or chrome sun, distant palm or pyramid silhouettes, and the [MOOD ELEMENT] floating tastefully; render the whole frame in saturated pink, cyan and purple with soft VHS bloom, faint scanlines and a hazy chromatic-aberration edge for that CRT-photographed feel; keep the subject clearly the hero, lit by the neon ambience. CONSTRAINTS: era-accurate 80s palette only (no muddy modern gradients), tasteful not chaotic, keep the subject recognizable, no readable logos, optional faint Japanese-style katakana decoration only if it stays decorative. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished vaporwave image only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to swap the palette or the mood element.

Title: Faded 1970s Film Photograph
Teaser: Makes any scene look like it was shot on expired Kodachrome in 1974 and left in a shoebox — warm amber cast, soft grain, slightly faded blacks, gentle light leak in the corner and that unmistakable analog softness. The found-photo nostalgia that hits instantly.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT/SCENE] to photograph, optionally set a [SETTING] (e.g. sunlit kitchen, roadside motel) and the [LIGHT] (e.g. warm afternoon, overcast).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a film photographer shooting on a vintage 35mm SLR with expired 1970s color stock. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT/SCENE] in a [SETTING] under [LIGHT] so it reads as a genuine faded 70s snapshot. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the casual amateur framing, the warm color cast the aged film would give, and where a soft light leak or vignette would naturally fall. THEN GENERATE: render the scene with warm amber-and-mustard tones, slightly lifted blacks, gentle desaturation and a faded highlight roll-off; add fine organic film grain, soft focus falloff, a subtle corner light leak and faint halation around bright spots; compose it like a real handheld family photo of the era — slightly off-center, honest, unposed. CONSTRAINTS: keep it photoreal (not illustrated), era-true 70s warmth (no teal-orange modern grade), believable period clothing, hair and objects, no text or watermarks, no perfect studio lighting. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished film-look photo only in a 3:2 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or push the fade stronger or weaker.

Title: Risograph Print Poster
Teaser: Reimagines your idea as a 2-color risograph print — those punchy spot inks (fluorescent pink + blue), visible halftone dots, slight misregistration and the matte recycled-paper texture that indie studios obsess over. The handmade-zine look you can't fake with filters.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT] to illustrate, optionally pick the [TWO INKS] (e.g. fluoro pink + teal, orange + navy) and a [PAPER] (e.g. cream, kraft).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a risograph printmaker designing a limited-run 2-color print. TASK: Illustrate [SUBJECT] as an authentic risograph poster using only [TWO INKS] on [PAPER]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the two ink layers overlap to create a third blended tone, which shapes carry each color, and where intentional misregistration adds charm. THEN GENERATE: build a bold, simplified illustration in just those two spot colors, layering flat shapes and visible coarse halftone dot patterns; add a slight 1-2px misregistration between the layers, subtle ink texture and grain, and the warm matte tooth of the chosen recycled paper showing through; keep the composition graphic and poster-like with generous flat areas. CONSTRAINTS: strictly two ink colors plus the paper (no full-color rendering, no smooth gradients except via halftone), keep it flat and screenprint-honest, leave clean space for an optional headline, no photoreal shading, no extra logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished risograph print only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to swap the two inks or the paper stock.

CONSTRAINTS: keep the analog degradation believable and not overdone, era-true 80s clothing and objects, the overlay text small and corner-placed, no modern UI, no watermark, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished VHS still only in a 4:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the scene or the timestamp.

Title: Retro Pixel Art Game Scene (8/16-bit)

PromptHint: Describe the [SCENE/SUBJECT] to build, optionally set the [BIT ERA] (e.g. 8-bit NES, 16-bit SNES) and a [SETTING] (e.g. forest level, neon city).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a retro game pixel artist building a frame for a [BIT ERA] cartridge title. TASK: Create a pixel-art game scene of [SCENE/SUBJECT] in a [SETTING], true to the [BIT ERA] hardware. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the tile-based composition, the tightly limited palette that era's console allowed, and how dithering stands in for gradients. THEN GENERATE: render crisp, chunky pixels on a clean grid with a small era-accurate palette, dithered shading, clear readable sprites and a parallax-layered background; add a simple faux HUD bar (score, hearts, generic icons) drawn in matching pixel style; keep the look sharp and aliased like real low-res sprite art, with strong silhouettes and limited colors per sprite. CONSTRAINTS: authentic low-resolution pixel grid (no anti-aliased smoothness, no high-res detail sneaking in), palette and sprite size true to the chosen era, readable and game-like, no real game logos or copyrighted characters, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished pixel-art scene only in a 4:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or the bit era.
